---
title: "Smart Amazon Product Query Assistant"
code-tools: false
description: "An AI-powered tool for enhancing the Amazon product search experience with natural language processing and machine learning."
date: 2026-04-19
categories: [NLP, Machine Learning, E-commerce, Python, BM25, Semantic Search, FAISS, Streamlit]
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: lux
---

### The value of this project to me

- **Technical skills**:
    - implementing and comparing three retrieval approaches (BM25, semantic search, hybrid) on a large real-world product dataset.
    - building a full RAG pipeline, from retrieval through context construction to LLM generation, with a Streamlit chat interface.
    - working with vector search (FAISS) and sentence embeddings, plus comparing two different LLMs for the generation step.

- **Collaboration**:
    - working with a teammate across two milestones, dividing work between retrieval methods and the generation/app layer.

- **Project management**:
    - structuring the project as incremental milestones, each with its own discussion and evaluation writeup, and documenting setup so the app can be reproduced from a fresh environment.

---

### Overview

This project is a context-aware product search assistant for Amazon listings. Given a natural language query, it retrieves relevant products and can generate a conversational answer grounded in product metadata and reviews. It was built for **UBC Master of Data Science, DSCI 575 Advanced Machine Learning course**, with teammate Harrison Li.

### Project repository

**GitHub repo:** [ubc-dsci575-smart-amazon-product-query-assistant](https://github.com/canadasung/ubc-dsci575-smart-amazon-product-query-assistant.git){target="_blank"} (mirrored from school repo because it is not publicly accessible)

---

### Dataset

- **Source**: [Amazon Reviews 2023](https://huggingface.co/datasets/McAuley-Lab/Amazon-Reviews-2023){target="_blank"} dataset (McAuley-Lab, via Hugging Face), Appliances category.
- **Scale**: 94,319 products, 2,128,605 total rows of product metadata (titles, descriptions, features, pricing) and aggregated user reviews.

---

### Architecture

The project progressed through two milestones, layering generation on top of retrieval.

#### Milestone 1: Retrieval methods

- **BM25**: traditional keyword matching, with scores normalized via max scaling.
- **Semantic search**: dense embeddings from `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (sentence-transformers), indexed in FAISS using `IndexFlatIP` for L2-normalized inner product (cosine similarity) search.
- **Hybrid retrieval**: combines both methods by normalizing each score to [0, 1] and blending them, `hybrid_score = alpha * norm_sem + (1 - alpha) * norm_bm25`, with alpha defaulting to 0.5.

#### Milestone 2: RAG pipeline

- **Workflow**: user query, retriever selection, document retrieval, context building, LLM generation, response shown in the chat UI.
- **Primary model**: `Llama-3.3-70b-versatile` via the Groq API, chosen for strong instruction-following and summarization, and for its large context window to handle the "lost in the middle" problem with lengthy product reviews. Groq's LPU hardware also gives fast inference.
- **Alternative model**: `gpt-oss-20b`, included for side-by-side comparison against the primary model.

---

### App

The assistant runs as a Streamlit chat app (`app/app.py`). Users pick a retrieval method and LLM, ask a question in natural language, and get back a grounded answer along with the retrieved products. LLM features require a `.env` file with `GROQ_API_KEY` and `TAVILY_API_KEY` (the latter for web search augmentation). A demo video [app-demo.mp4](https://github.com/canadasung/ubc-dsci575-smart-amazon-product-query-assistant/blob/main/app-demo.mp4){target="_blank"} walks through the app in the repo.

---

### Installation & usage

```bash
git clone https://github.com/canadasung/DSCI_575_project_hli76_wnsong.git
cd DSCI_575_project_hli76_wnsong
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate <env-name>
python src/download_data.py
streamlit run app/app.py
```

The data pipeline produces raw parquet files, processed documents and product indices, a BM25 pickle index with tokenized corpus, and a FAISS vector index for semantic search. User feedback collected from the app is logged to CSV.

---

### Results & evaluation

Detailed findings and evaluation discussion for each milestone are documented in the repo under [results/milestone1_discussion.md](https://github.com/canadasung/DSCI_575_project_hli76_wnsong/blob/main/results/milestone1_discussion.md){target="_blank"} and [results/milestone2_discussion.md](https://github.com/canadasung/DSCI_575_project_hli76_wnsong/blob/main/results/milestone2_discussion.md){target="_blank"}.

---

### Key takeaways

- **Retrieval choice matters**: BM25, semantic search, and hybrid retrieval each have different strengths, and blending them gave more flexibility than committing to one method.
- **Model size versus speed tradeoff**: comparing a 70B and a 20B model surfaced practical tradeoffs between answer quality and inference cost/speed, which Groq's hardware helped offset.
